1.text: Abib was the first month of the ancient Hebrew lunar calendar, usually occurring within the months of March and April; twice in this verse.

10.text: That is, Shavuot; see footnotes for verse 16.

13.text: Or Booths or Shelters ; see footnotes for verse 16.

16.text: That is, the seven-day period after the Passover during which no leaven may be eaten; see Exodus 12:14–20.
That is, Shavuot, the late spring feast of pilgrimage to Jerusalem; it is also known as the Feast of Harvest (see Exodus 23:16) or the Feast of Pentecost (see Acts 2:1).

That is, Sukkot, the autumn feast of pilgrimage to Jerusalem; also translated as the Feast of Booths or the Feast of Shelters and originally called the Feast of Ingathering (see Exodus 23:16 and Exodus 34:22).
